title: minaria volubilis
intro: Minaria volubilis, also known as the 'firecracker vine', is a flowering plant native to Brazil in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which are attributed to the presence of bufadienolides, a group of cardiac glycosides. The health benefits of Minaria volubilis include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its use in treating cardiovascular conditions. Minaria volubilis can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine for its antispasmodic and expectorant properties.
